body:
Hey @styfle,
The CI failure in test prod (9/10) is unrelated to this PR. The failing test is test/e2e/app-dir/concurrent-navigations/mismatching-prefetch.test.ts ,its a Playwright/router-act test that timed out after 60s with no assertion reached. It shares no codepath with packages/next/src/server/image-optimizer.ts.

I have verified locally: test/unit/image-optimizer/fetch-external-image.test.ts passes with both Turbopack and IS_WEBPACK_TEST=1 NEXT_TEST_MODE=start. Could you please re-run the failed job once?
author: rishishanbhag

author: rishishanbhag

body:
Hey @styfle,

The new CI failure (test prod 2/10) is also unrelated. The failing test is test/e2e/app-dir/app-prefetch/prefetching.test.ts - zero codepath overlap with image-optimizer.ts (verified by grep across the entire test directory).

A few data points confirming it's a known flake:

Upstream PR #91734 ("Unflake app prefetch test") explicitly targets this file: "Another victim of the Playwright upgrade."
The test still contains a hard expect(timeToComplete).toBeLessThan(1000) wall-clock assertion (line 56) which is fragile under production-shard CI load.
The job shows IS_RETRY=true, meaning CI already auto-retried once — consistent with a timing flake under worker pressure.


Would you prefer that I:
1. Investigate further into this test failure, or  
2. Wait for CI stabilization / another re-run?

Happy to dig deeper if needed.
author: rishishanbhag

body: Also hitting this on Next.js 16.2.1 / Node.js 24.11.1. Exact crash with `RangeError: Map maximum size exceeded` at `AsyncHook.init` in `app-page-turbo.runtime.dev.js`, triggered by the hot-reloader server-chunk subscriptions after multiple simultaneous Turbopack compilation timeouts. Downgrading to 16.1.6 as a temporary workaround. Waiting for this fix to land.

body:
Clean fix. One thing worth noting: the `startsWith('file://')` check is case-sensitive, so a `FILE://` URL would fall through to `path.join` and produce a broken result. In practice Node.js and browsers always emit lowercase `file://`, so this is an edge case, but worth a comment or lowercased comparison if you want to be defensive.

Also, a test covering a Windows-style `file:///` URL passed as `filePath` directly (e.g. `file:///C:/app/handler.js`) would make the pass-through coverage explicit alongside the existing Windows path test.
author: afurm

body:
@afurm Thanks for the thorough review! I've addressed both of your suggestions:

1. **Case-insensitive check**: Changed  to  and added a comment explaining that Node.js and browsers always emit lowercase 'file://'.

2. **Windows URL tests**: Added two new test cases:
   -  - verifies that uppercase FILE:// URLs are passed through correctly
   -  - explicitly tests Windows-style file:// URLs

The changes ensure robust handling of file:// URLs across different platforms and casing scenarios. Please take another look when you have a chance!
author: wdskuki

## Failing test suites <!--61965781752-->

author: nextjs-bot

body:
It looks like this test is failing to run in CI because multiple bundler environment variables are set (see the CI logs). You'll need to unset the webpack environment variable.

This test is a bit unique because we don't have any other "rspack-specific" tests in the repo. Instead, we just run the webpack tests with a different mode nightly (https://www.arewerspackyet.com/), but because of CI capacity and (to a lesser degree) maintenance ownership reasons, we don't run the full suite with rspack on every PR.
author: bgw

body:
> It looks like this test is failing to run in CI because multiple bundler environment variables are set (see the CI logs). You'll need to unset the webpack environment variable.

Instead, we just run the webpack tests with a different mode nightly (https://www.arewerspackyet.com/), but because of CI capacity and (to a lesser degree) maintenance ownership reasons, we don't run the full suite with rspack on every PR.

I’ve applied the changes in [38e3a2a](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/91784/commits/38e3a2a8e2745c3163367ab50657635d7573ee11). Could you take a look and see if it looks ok?
I don’t want to keep bothering you, but it’s a bit hard to fully grasp the context related with webpack or rspack which run on github action here.
or Would you recommend skipping tests for this change?

body: @stormslowly rspack resolver should pass `.pnp.cjs` file path into file dependencies.

body: @NaamuKim I think this is a bug in the Rspack resolver: after resolving, it doesn't add `.pnp.cjs` to `fileDependencies`.
author: SyMind

body:
> @NaamuKim I think this is a bug in the Rspack resolver: after resolving, it doesn't add `.pnp.cjs` to `fileDependencies`.

i will fix it.
author: stormslowly

body:
Thanks. I don’t really mind where it gets resolved.
Let me know if you need anything.
Also, using the Yarn Berry API to trigger revalidation only when related dependencies change within the monorepo seems like a solid approach.

body:
### Screenshots

**Typo in `qualities` section** — "If not configuration" should be "If no configuration":
![qualities typo](https://files.catbox.moe/8ztmu4.png)

**Version History table** — v16.0.0 row is missing `minimumCacheTTL`, `imageSizes`, and `localPatterns.search` breaking changes:
![version history](https://files.catbox.moe/cq0w9g.png)
author: mvanhorn

body: Checking in on this docs fix. It corrects the qualities typo and fills in the v16 version history entries. Screenshots in the PR show the before/after. Happy to rebase onto canary if that helps.
author: mvanhorn

body: Good fix. One minor nit: the default `['js', 'jsx', 'ts', 'tsx']` is repeated across `parseUrlForPages`, `parseUrlForAppDir`, `getUrlFromPagesDirectories`, `getUrlFromAppDirectory`, and the rule fallback. Worth extracting to a shared constant to avoid drift if the defaults ever change.
author: afurm

body: @afurm Great point. I've refactored the code to extract the default extensions to a shared `ALLOWED_PAGE_EXTENSIONS` constant in `utils/url.ts` to avoid duplication and keep it maintainable. Thanks!
author: cyphercodes

body:
Pushed a small follow-up to fix the CI type error from the shared default extension refactor.

`context.settings.next` is typed as `unknown` by the current ESLint types, so the rule now narrows it locally before reading `pageExtensions` and falling back to `ALLOWED_PAGE_EXTENSIONS`.

Local verification:
- `pnpm --filter @next/eslint-plugin-next build` ✅
- `pnpm lint-eslint packages/eslint-plugin-next/src/rules/no-html-link-for-pages.ts packages/eslint-plugin-next/src/utils/url.ts` ✅
- `pnpm prettier --check packages/eslint-plugin-next/src/rules/no-html-link-for-pages.ts packages/eslint-plugin-next/src/utils/url.ts` ✅
- `pnpm jest test/unit/eslint-plugin-next/no-html-link-for-pages.test.ts --runInBand` ✅

Note: the new GitHub Actions runs for this fork commit are currently `action_required` and need maintainer approval to run.
author: cyphercodes

body:
Addressed the Vercel bot note in `parseUrlForAppDir` by making nested app-dir recursion call `parseUrlForAppDir` instead of `parseUrlForPages`.

Verification run:
- `pnpm --filter @next/eslint-plugin-next build` ✅
- `pnpm lint-eslint packages/eslint-plugin-next/src/rules/no-html-link-for-pages.ts packages/eslint-plugin-next/src/utils/url.ts` ✅
- `pnpm prettier --check packages/eslint-plugin-next/src/rules/no-html-link-for-pages.ts packages/eslint-plugin-next/src/utils/url.ts` ✅
- `pnpm jest --config /tmp/next-pr-jest.config.cjs test/unit/eslint-plugin-next/no-html-link-for-pages.test.ts --runInBand` ✅ (temporary SWC-only config used because default repo Jest requires prebuilt `packages/next/dist` in this shallow checkout)
author: cyphercodes
